summary: Brazil uses 8-digit NCM codes shared across Mercosur countries (Argentina, Paraguay, Uruguay). NCM is divided into 21 sections and 99 chapters.

data_availability.format: Not directly downloadable as CSV; requires API or manual compilation

sources.name: Portal Único Siscomex
sources.description: Brazil's unified customs portal for NCM information

sources.name: Receita Federal
sources.description: Brazilian tax authority maintaining NCM classifications

sources.name: Export Genius NCM Database
sources.description: Searchable NCM code database with updated 2026 data

sources.name: SEAIR HS Code Database
sources.description: Brazilian HS codes and trade data

sources.name: NOVATRADE - NCM Information
sources.description: Detailed information on NCM classification and revision procedures

mercosur_alignment.note: First 6 digits are Harmonized System (HS) standard. Digits 7-8 are Mercosur-specific

tariff_rates.access: Via MDIC (Ministry of Development, Industry and Foreign Trade) or customs brokers
tariff_rates.note: Variable by product category and origin. Requires consultation with official sources.

next_steps: Contact Receita Federal directly for bulk NCM code export
Access Portal Único Siscomex with credentials for detailed tariff information
Compile data from Export Genius or SEAIR commercial databases
Cross-reference with MDIC tariff database for import duty rates
notes: Complete 8-digit NCM database is maintained by Receita Federal but not freely available as downloadable CSV. Third-party databases maintain updated copies but may require subscription.
